goal: Land the G7 (Quality) parallel-pipeline backbone — `G7Source` trait + `execute_g7` orchestrator + severity-weighted merge fusion (`merge_g7_by_severity`) — alongside `quality_issues`-table trend-tracking (UPSERT preserving `first_seen`, `last_seen` updates, resolved transitions, soft-delete after retention), wiring P3-W11-F01/F05/F06 around the shared §3.2 row 14 (`check_quality`) + §5.7 (G7 severity-weighted merge) + §12.1 (quality_issues schema) contract per the QUINTUPLE-validated bundle-coordinated-features template (WO-0067/0068/0070/0083/0084).
plan_summary: Mirror the proven G3 (WO-0070) and G4 (WO-0083) pattern for G7: create `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g7.rs` housing `G7Source` trait + `G7Issue` data + `G7SourceStatus` + `G7SourceOutput` + `G7Outcome` + `execute_g7` orchestrator (parallel fan-out under per-source + master deadlines) + a `Severity` enum (`Critical`/`High`/`Medium`/`Low`/`Info`, `derive(PartialOrd, Ord)` with `Critical = 0` so the natural `Ord` puts the most-severe-first via `min()`) + `MergedG7Issue` + `pub fn merge_g7_by_severity` (dedup by `(file_path, line, category)`, keep highest severity within group, sort overall output severity-descending). Production `LspDiagnosticsG7Source` impl is deferred per DEC-0008 §4 dependency-inversion seam — the frozen `g7::test_g7_parallel_pipeline` uses an in-process `TestG7Source` impl identical in shape to WO-0070's `TestG3Source`. F06 ships as a focused edit to `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/quality_pipeline.rs`: `persist_diagnostics` switches to SELECT-then-UPSERT (preserve `first_seen`, update `last_seen`, transition `resolved=1` for rows in the same file no longer in the current observation set), and `pub async fn soft_delete_resolved_quality_issues(kg, now: SystemTime, retention_days: u32)` is added to delete rows with `resolved=1 AND last_seen older than now - retention_days`. Three pre-baked mutation contracts (M1 per-source-timeout bypass, M2 severity-precedence inversion, M3 `first_seen` overwrite on re-observation) all detected by frozen module-root tests, restored via `git checkout --` + md5sum verify.
scope_in: 1. Net-new file `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g7.rs` for the F01 + F05 surfaces. Module rustdoc cites master-plan §3.2 row 14 (`check_quality` G7-routed), §5.7 (G7: Quality — All quality tools → severity-weighted merge — verbatim block at lines 539-559), §6.1 (cross-group parallel pipeline shape), §15.2 (`ucil.group.quality` tracing span name), §17 line 1636 (canonical `g7.rs` directory placement), §18 Phase 3 Week 11 deliverable #1 (LSP diagnostics bridge as G7) and #2 (severity-weighted merge fusion). Module imports: `std::pin::Pin`, `std::time::Duration`, `std::future::{poll_fn, Future}`, `std::task::Poll`, `serde::{Deserialize, Serialize}`, `async_trait::async_trait`. The file MUST set `#![deny(rustdoc::broken_intra_doc_links)]` per `.claude/rules/rust-style.md`. Mirror the structure of `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g3.rs` verbatim where the contract overlaps (orchestrator shape, per-source timeout helper, fan-out join helper, status enum). Cite WO-0070 + WO-0083 precedent in the module rustdoc (`Same shape as WO-0070 G3 / WO-0083 G4`).

2. In `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g7.rs`, add `pub const G7_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE: Duration = Duration::from_millis(4500);` and `pub const G7_DEFAULT_MASTER_DEADLINE: Duration = Duration::from_millis(5500);` (mirror the G3 constant naming + values). Doc each constant inline citing master-plan §15 (timeouts) and the WO-0068/WO-0070 precedent for the unconditional-per-source-deadline shape (NOT min'd with master_deadline — see WO-0068 §planner #4 lessons).

3. In `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g7.rs`, add `pub struct G7Query { pub target: String, pub categories: Vec<String> }` with `#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]`. `target` is the file path or symbol the quality query is anchored on; `categories` filters which issue categories to emit (`type_error` / `lint` / `security` / `style` / `complexity` per §12.1) — empty `Vec` means all categories. Doc comment citing §3.2 row 14 (`check_quality(target, type)`).

4. In `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g7.rs`, add `pub enum Severity { Critical, High, Medium, Low, Info }` with `#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]` AND `#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]`. Discriminant order MUST be `Critical = 0` < `High = 1` < `Medium = 2` < `Low = 3` < `Info = 4` so the natural `Ord` gives `Critical < High < Medium < Low < Info` — the LOWEST discriminant is the MOST severe (mirror the §5.7 line 555 hierarchy `critical > high > medium > low` and the §12.1 column legend; the merge function uses `.min()` to keep the most severe). Doc comment cites §5.7 lines 555-556 verbatim. Add a `pub const fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str` returning the lowercase string for `quality_issues.severity` column projection (`"critical"`, `"high"`, `"medium"`, `"low"`, `"info"`).

5. In `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g7.rs`, add `pub struct G7Issue { pub source_tool: String, pub file_path: String, pub line_start: Option<u32>, pub line_end: Option<u32>, pub category: String, pub severity: Severity, pub message: String, pub rule_id: Option<String>, pub fix_suggestion: Option<String> }` with `#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]`. Field-by-field maps to the §12.1 `quality_issues` columns (no `id` / `first_seen` / `last_seen` / `resolved` / `resolved_by_session` — those are persistence-side concerns owned by F06's `persist_diagnostics` UPSERT, NOT the in-memory issue surface).

6. In `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g7.rs`, add `pub enum G7SourceStatus { Available, TimedOut, Errored }` with `#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]` (verbatim same shape as `G3SourceStatus`). Add `pub struct G7SourceOutput { pub source_id: String, pub status: G7SourceStatus, pub elapsed_ms: u64, pub issues: Vec<G7Issue>, pub error: Option<String> }` with `#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]`. Add `pub struct G7Outcome { pub results: Vec<G7SourceOutput>, pub wall_elapsed_ms: u64, pub master_timed_out: bool }` with `#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]`. The `results` order matches the input `sources` index per WO-0070 precedent.

7. In `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g7.rs`, add the `G7Source` trait per DEC-0008 §4 dependency-inversion seam: `#[async_trait] pub trait G7Source: Send + Sync { fn source_id(&self) -> &str; async fn execute(&self, query: &G7Query) -> G7SourceOutput; }`. The trait is UCIL-owned — NOT a re-export of any external wire format — production `LspDiagnosticsG7Source` impl is deferred to a follow-up production-wiring WO that bundles the daemon-startup-orchestration registration (see scope_out #1). Doc rustdoc cites WO-0047 G1Source / WO-0070 G3Source / WO-0083 G4Source precedent.

8. In `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g7.rs`, add `async fn run_g7_source(source: &(dyn G7Source + Send + Sync), query: &G7Query, per_source_deadline: Duration) -> G7SourceOutput` mirroring `run_g3_source` (per-source `tokio::time::timeout` converting elapse into `G7SourceStatus::TimedOut` + non-empty `error` carrying `"per-source deadline N ms exceeded"`). Add `#[allow(dead_code)] async fn join_all_g7<T>(futures: Vec<Pin<Box<dyn Future<Output = T> + Send + 'a>>>) -> Vec<T>` mirroring `join_all_g3` verbatim (the `#[allow(dead_code)]` is required so M1 mutation — replacing the parallel join with sequential `.await` — does NOT trigger `#![deny(warnings)]` per WO-0070 §executor lessons). Inline rustdoc on `join_all_g7` MUST cite the M1 mutation contract.

9. In `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g7.rs`, add `pub async fn execute_g7(sources: Vec<Box<dyn G7Source + Send + Sync + 'static>>, query: G7Query, master_deadline: Duration) -> G7Outcome` with `#[tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.group.quality", skip(sources, query), fields(source_count = sources.len(), master_deadline_ms = master_deadline.as_millis() as u64))]` per master-plan §15.2 line 1519. The orchestrator: (a) records start `Instant`; (b) builds per-source futures via `run_g7_source(source, &query, G7_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE)`; (c) wraps the join in `tokio::time::timeout(master_deadline, join_all_g7(futures))`; (d) on master-trip synthesises in-order `G7SourceStatus::TimedOut` placeholders so `results[i].source_id == sources[i].source_id()` either way (mirror G3); (e) returns `G7Outcome { results, wall_elapsed_ms, master_timed_out }`. Public API rustdoc carries an `# Examples` ignore-doctest constructing a `Vec<Box<dyn G7Source + ...>>` from a hypothetical `MyG7Source { id }` (NO need to construct a real `LspDiagnosticsG7Source` since production impls are deferred). All `pub` items in g7.rs that are non-trivial MUST carry rustdoc per `.claude/rules/rust-style.md` `# Examples` requirement.

10. In `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g7.rs`, add the F05 surface: `pub struct MergedG7Issue { pub file_path: String, pub line_start: Option<u32>, pub category: String, pub severity: Severity, pub message: String, pub source_tools: Vec<String>, pub rule_ids: Vec<String>, pub fix_suggestions: Vec<String> }` with `#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]`. The `source_tools` / `rule_ids` / `fix_suggestions` Vecs are the deduplicated alphabetically-sorted union across all merged `G7Issue` instances at the same `(file_path, line_start, category)` group (the §5.7 line 556 directive `keep highest-severity with merged details`).

11. In `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g7.rs`, add `pub fn merge_g7_by_severity(issues: &[G7Issue]) -> Vec<MergedG7Issue>` with `#[must_use]`. Empty input returns `vec![]`. Group `issues` by `(file_path, line_start, category)` (use `BTreeMap<(String, Option<u32>, String), Vec<&G7Issue>>` for deterministic ordering). Within each group, find the most-severe `Severity` via `.iter().map(|i| i.severity).min().unwrap()` (smallest discriminant = most severe). Among the candidates carrying the highest severity in the group, the FIRST one (ordered by source_tool ASC) is selected for `severity` / `message` / `line_start` / `category` / `file_path`. The `source_tools` / `rule_ids` / `fix_suggestions` vecs project the deduplicated alphabetically-sorted union over ALL issues in the group (not just the highest-severity ones — the §5.7 line 556 `merged details` directive). The output is sorted: primary key = `severity` ascending (which is `Critical` first per the discriminant order), secondary = `file_path` ASC, tertiary = `line_start` ASC (NULL/None last). Doc comment cites §5.7 verbatim.

12. Frozen `#[cfg(test)] pub async fn test_g7_parallel_pipeline()` at MODULE ROOT in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g7.rs` (NOT inside `mod tests { ... }`). Selector substring-match `cargo test -p ucil-daemon g7::test_g7_parallel_pipeline` MUST resolve uniquely (no `--exact`). Test attribute `#[tokio::test]`. The test exercises three sources (`TestG7Source` impls inside `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` — NOT mocks of any external system, but in-process trait impls per DEC-0008 §4): `available` (returns 1 issue immediately, status=Available), `slow` (sleeps 4700ms, would-be issues never delivered, status MUST become TimedOut once per-source deadline trips), `errored` (returns status=Errored with non-empty `error`, no issues). Use `master_deadline = Duration::from_millis(5500)`. SA-numbered assertions: (SA1) `outcome.results.len() == 3`; (SA2) `outcome.master_timed_out == false` (master deadline does NOT trip); (SA3) `outcome.results[0].status == G7SourceStatus::Available && outcome.results[0].issues.len() == 1`; (SA4) `outcome.results[1].status == G7SourceStatus::TimedOut && outcome.results[1].issues.is_empty() && outcome.results[1].error.as_deref().unwrap_or("").contains("per-source deadline")`; (SA5) `outcome.results[2].status == G7SourceStatus::Errored && outcome.results[2].error.is_some()`; (SA6) `outcome.wall_elapsed_ms < 5000` (parallel — `slow` source's 4500ms timeout dominates, but no further serialisation; sequential 200ms+4700ms+200ms = 5100+ would slip through SA6 only if the executor accidentally sequentialised, hence SA6 is non-ceremonial). Each `assert_eq!`/`assert!` carries panic body `(SAn) <semantic name>; left: ..., right: ...` per DEC-0007 + WO-0066/0067/0068/0070/0083/0084 mature pattern.

13. Frozen `#[cfg(test)] pub fn test_g7_severity_merge()` at MODULE ROOT in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g7.rs` (NOT inside `mod tests { ... }`). Selector substring-match `cargo test -p ucil-daemon g7::test_g7_severity_merge` MUST resolve uniquely (no `--exact`). Sub-scenarios with SA-numbered panic messages: (SA1) empty input → `merge_g7_by_severity(&[]) == vec![]`; (SA2) single-issue input → output preserves all fields verbatim with `source_tools=vec![issue.source_tool.clone()]`; (SA3) two issues at SAME `(file_path, line_start, category)` with severities `Medium` and `Critical` → merged keeps `severity=Critical`, `source_tools` is alphabetical-sorted union of both source_tools; (SA4) three issues spanning two distinct `(file_path, line_start, category)` groups with severities {Group A: Critical+Low; Group B: High} → output has 2 entries, sorted severity-ascending = Critical first then High (NOT Low — the high-severity win in Group A); (SA5) tied-severity merge: two issues at SAME group both `High`, source_tools alphabetically `aaa` and `bbb` → merged carries `message`/`rule_id`/`fix_suggestion` from the FIRST candidate by source_tool ASC (`aaa`), `source_tools=vec!["aaa", "bbb"]` (deduped + sorted), AND `rule_ids` / `fix_suggestions` show the deduplicated union if both issues have non-None values; (SA6) sentinel-row: a `vec![Critical, Critical, High, Medium, Low, Info]`-mix across distinct groups returns 6 outputs sorted severity-ascending (Critical, Critical, High, Medium, Low, Info) with the canonical lowercase `severity.as_str()` mapping `"critical"/"high"/"medium"/"low"/"info"` — the master-plan §5.7 vocabulary canary (analogous to WO-0067 §6.2 sentinel-row). Each `assert_eq!`/`assert!` carries panic body `(SAn) <semantic name>; left: ..., right: ...`.

14. F01 + F05 module wiring: `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs` adds exactly ONE line: `pub mod g7;` placed alphabetically between `pub mod g4;` (line 160) and `pub mod lancedb_indexer;` (line 161). NO `pub use` re-exports introduced (consumers reach types via `ucil_daemon::g7::*` directly per WO-0084 lib.rs convention). The added line is the ONLY edit to `lib.rs` for F01+F05.

15. F06 surface in `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/quality_pipeline.rs`: rewrite `persist_diagnostics` to use SELECT-then-UPSERT semantics. Within the existing `kg.execute_in_transaction(|tx| { ... })` block, REPLACE the simple `INSERT INTO quality_issues ...` pattern with: (a) prepare a SELECT statement `SELECT id FROM quality_issues WHERE file_path = ?1 AND COALESCE(line_start, -1) = COALESCE(?2, -1) AND category = ?3 AND severity = ?4 AND message = ?5 LIMIT 1`; (b) for each `QualityIssueRow`, run the SELECT first; (c) if a row id is found, run `UPDATE quality_issues SET last_seen = datetime('now'), resolved = 0 WHERE id = ?1` (does NOT touch `first_seen`); (d) if no row is found, run the existing INSERT (the `first_seen` column has `DEFAULT (datetime('now'))` already; explicitly include `last_seen = datetime('now')` so SA `last_seen IS NOT NULL` holds on first observation). The dedup key is `(file_path, line_start, category, severity, message)` per master-plan §12.1 row uniqueness; rule_id and source_tool are NOT in the dedup key (different rule_ids on the same line+severity+message would be a contradiction; pinning them in the dedup key would split otherwise-identical issues).

16. F06 resolve-transition: in the same `execute_in_transaction` block of `persist_diagnostics`, AFTER the INSERT/UPDATE pass, run a single statement `UPDATE quality_issues SET resolved = 1 WHERE file_path = ?1 AND resolved = 0 AND id NOT IN (...rebuilt-id-set...)` to mark as resolved any row in the same `file_path` that was NOT in the current observation set. Pass the file_path AS a single parameter; pass the surviving id list either via a `?` placeholder array (rusqlite supports `params_from_iter`) or via a temporary table-style approach. The simpler shape: track `Vec<i64>` of touched ids during step (c)/(d) above, then run `UPDATE ... WHERE file_path = ?1 AND resolved = 0 AND id NOT IN (id1, id2, ...)` via `format!`-built SQL with bound params. (Per master-plan §12.1 + the F06 description.) Edge case: when `diagnostics.is_empty()` AND existing `quality_issues` rows for `file_path` exist with `resolved = 0`, those rows MUST be transitioned to `resolved = 1`. The current early-return-on-empty in `persist_diagnostics` (line 343-346) MUST be removed OR augmented to still run the resolve-transition step. (Recommendation: remove the early-return so empty diagnostics correctly resolve all outstanding rows in the file.)

17. F06 soft-delete helper: add `pub async fn soft_delete_resolved_quality_issues(kg: &mut KnowledgeGraph, now: SystemTime, retention_days: u32) -> Result<usize, QualityPipelineError>` to `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/quality_pipeline.rs`. The function: (a) computes the cutoff timestamp as `now - retention_days * 24 * 3600` and renders it as an SQLite-comparable `TEXT` (`format!("{}-{}-{} {}:{}:{}", ...)` from `OffsetDateTime::from_unix_timestamp` — but to keep deps minimal, use `SystemTime::duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)` + a hand-rolled UTC formatter, OR pull a single-line `chrono` shim if `chrono` is already in `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/Cargo.toml`; executor MUST check `cargo metadata --format-version 1 -p ucil-lsp-diagnostics | jq '.packages[0].dependencies[] | select(.name=="chrono")'` BEFORE deciding); (b) inside `kg.execute_in_transaction(|tx| { ... })`, runs `DELETE FROM quality_issues WHERE resolved = 1 AND last_seen IS NOT NULL AND last_seen < ?1` with the rendered cutoff as the bound parameter; (c) returns the deleted-row count via `tx.changes()` (or the executed-statement's affected-rows, propagated as `usize`). Function carries `#[tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.lsp.soft_delete_resolved_quality_issues", ...)]` per §15.2.

18. F06 frozen test: in `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/quality_pipeline.rs`, add `#[cfg(test)] pub async fn test_quality_issues_tracking()` at MODULE ROOT (NOT inside `mod tests { ... }`). Selector substring-match `cargo test -p ucil-lsp-diagnostics quality_pipeline::test_quality_issues_tracking` MUST resolve uniquely. Test attribute `#[tokio::test]`. The test: (a) opens a `KnowledgeGraph` via the existing `test_fixtures::open_kg(...)` helper (per the existing module convention); (b) sets up a `fake_serena_client::FakeSerenaClient` with a fixed diagnostic D1 for file F1; (c) runs `persist_diagnostics(client, kg, F1.uri(), Language::Rust)` once → SA1 `inserted == 1`, row 1 has `first_seen != NULL`, `last_seen != NULL`, `resolved == 0`; (d) re-runs `persist_diagnostics` with the SAME diagnostic → SA2 `inserted == 1` (UPDATE counts as 1 since the SELECT-then-UPDATE branch ran once), row 1's `first_seen` is UNCHANGED from step (c), `last_seen >= first_seen`, `resolved == 0`, AND `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM quality_issues WHERE file_path = F1` returns 1 (NOT 2 — the UPSERT did not insert a duplicate); (e) runs `persist_diagnostics` with EMPTY diagnostics for F1 → row 1 transitions to `resolved == 1` per the step-16 resolve-transition; (f) runs `soft_delete_resolved_quality_issues(kg, fake_now_8_days_later, 7)` → SA5 returns `1` (the resolved row past 7-day retention is deleted), `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM quality_issues` returns 0; (g) sentinel: re-run step (f) with `fake_now_5_days_later` → SA6 returns `0` (resolved row within retention is preserved). 6+ SA-numbered assertions, each with `(SAn) <semantic name>; left: ..., right: ...` panic bodies.

The `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block houses `TestG7Source` (NOT `MockG7Source` or `FakeG7Source` — the `Test` prefix is the canonical pattern per WO-0070's `TestG3Source` and WO-0083's `TestG4Source`). For `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/quality_pipeline.rs`, the existing `fake_serena_client` module is exempt under WO-0048 line 363's `#[cfg(test)]` carve-out + WO-0069 §executor lessons.

20. DEC-0005 module-coherence carve-out: the F01+F05 single feat commit lands `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g7.rs` (~700-900 LOC = orchestrator + types + trait + helper fns + module-root frozen tests + TestG7Source) AS ONE COHESIVE COMMIT, plus the `lib.rs` `pub mod g7;` declaration. Splitting helper-by-helper would produce stub-shaped intermediate states (e.g. `pub mod g7;` without the file would not compile; F01 trait without F05 merger would force an artificial cohesion split mid-file). The commit body MUST cite `DEC-0005 module-coherence` + WO-0067/0068/0070/0083/0084 LOC-precedent (548/788/720/982/995 LOC respectively). The F06 changes ship as a separate cohesive commit on the same branch.

21. Conventional Commits per `.claude/rules/commit-style.md`: each commit body carries `Phase: 3`, `Feature: P3-W11-F01` / `Feature: P3-W11-F05` / `Feature: P3-W11-F06`, `Work-order: WO-0085` trailers and the `Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>` trailer. Subject lines aim ≤70 chars (soft target). Recommended commit sequence: (a) `feat(daemon): add g7 quality pipeline orchestrator and severity merge` covering F01+F05 (single ~800-LOC commit per DEC-0005); (b) `test(daemon): cover g7 parallel pipeline and severity merge` if the test bodies were split out (otherwise rolled into (a)); (c) `feat(lsp-diagnostics): persist quality_issues with upsert and soft-delete` for F06; (d) `test(lsp-diagnostics): cover quality_issues first_seen + resolved-transition + soft-delete` if test was split out (otherwise rolled into (c)); (e) `chore(verify): scripts for P3-W11-F01/F05/F06`. The exact sequence may vary; the constraint is NEVER more than 4 commits and always cohesive per DEC-0005. Push after every commit per CLAUDE.md mandatory-cadence rule.

22. New verify scripts: `scripts/verify/P3-W11-F01.sh`, `scripts/verify/P3-W11-F05.sh`, `scripts/verify/P3-W11-F06.sh`. Each is a `#!/usr/bin/env bash` `set -euo pipefail` script that runs the relevant `cargo test` selector (substring-match), checks for the expected pub-API grep matches, runs `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` on the target crate, and `cargo fmt --check` on the target crate. Each MUST exit 0 in the green path and exit 1 if any sub-check fails. Mirror the structure of `scripts/verify/P3-W10-F10.sh` and `scripts/verify/P3-W10-F12.sh` (both shipped under WO-0084).

23. Crate-level `#![deny(warnings)]` + `#[allow(dead_code)]` on `join_all_g7` mirrors WO-0070 §executor lessons: the M1 mutation (sequential .await loop in execute_g7) leaves `join_all_g7` orphaned; without `#[allow(dead_code)]` the dead-code warning would be promoted to an error and the verifier would observe a compile failure instead of the SA-tagged panic. The `#[allow(dead_code)]` MUST carry an inline rustdoc citation of the M1 contract (per WO-0070 line 192 precedent).

24. Tracing carve-out for the F05 pure-deterministic merge function: `merge_g7_by_severity` is pure-deterministic CPU-bound logic — no async, no IO, no logging. Per WO-0067 §`lessons_applied` #5 and the WO-0070 G3 G3 fusion-side merger precedent, the §15.2 tracing-span requirement does NOT apply to pure-deterministic CPU-only fns. Disclose explicitly in the RFR's `## Disclosed deviations` section (it is NOT a deviation per se but a planner-anticipated carve-out). `execute_g7` (which IS async/IO/orchestration) DOES carry `#[tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.group.quality")]` per §15.2.

25. RFR (`ucil-build/work-orders/0085-ready-for-review.md`) MUST include sections: `## What I verified locally` (cargo-test/clippy/fmt/coverage/no-merge-commits/word-ban/grep), `## Mutation contract` (M1+M2+M3 with file/lines/patch/SA/restore/pre-md5 columns + observed panics), `## Test-type effectiveness` (which SA caught which mutation, ceremonial vs load-bearing), `## Disclosed deviations` (any spirit-over-literal SA tightenings, the tracing carve-out for `merge_g7_by_severity`, AC36/AC37 sub-session disclosed if applicable), `## Trace-span coverage` (confirmation of `ucil.group.quality` span on `execute_g7` per §15.2), `## DEC reference` (DEC-0005 + DEC-0007 + DEC-0008 §4 + the M1/M2/M3 contract). Mirror the WO-0084 0084-ready-for-review.md template.

26. Pre-baked M1 mutation contract (F01 per-source-timeout bypass): file `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g7.rs`, the line(s) inside `run_g7_source` where `tokio::time::timeout(per_source_deadline, source.execute(query))` is invoked. Patch: replace the `tokio::time::timeout(per_source_deadline, source.execute(query)).await.unwrap_or_else(|_| { ... })` block with a direct `source.execute(query).await` (fall-through Available output). Targeted SA: SA4 (`outcome.results[1].status == G7SourceStatus::TimedOut`) — the `slow` source returning Available after 4700ms instead of TimedOut after 4500ms. Restore: `git checkout -- crates/ucil-daemon/src/g7.rs`. Pre-md5 path: `/tmp/wo-0085-g7-orig.md5`. Verifier MUST take an md5 snapshot via `md5sum crates/ucil-daemon/src/g7.rs > /tmp/wo-0085-g7-orig.md5` BEFORE the first mutation.

27. Pre-baked M2 mutation contract (F05 severity-precedence inversion): file `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g7.rs`, the `merge_g7_by_severity` body's `.iter().map(|i| i.severity).min().unwrap()` call. Patch: change `.min().unwrap()` to `.max().unwrap()` (now picks the LEAST severe — Info — instead of the MOST severe — Critical). Targeted SA: SA3 (two issues at same group with severities Medium and Critical → merged.severity == Critical) — under the mutation, merged.severity == Medium (the `.max()` picks the higher discriminant = less severe). May ALSO trip SA4 / SA6 first depending on test-body assertion order; per WO-0070/0083/0084 spirit-over-literal precedent, ANY analogous severity-precedence-inversion failure mode is acceptable provided the executor discloses the actual-observed SA in the RFR.

28. Pre-baked M3 mutation contract (F06 first_seen overwrite on UPSERT update): file `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/quality_pipeline.rs`, the UPDATE statement built in step (c) of scope_in #15. Patch: change `UPDATE quality_issues SET last_seen = datetime('now'), resolved = 0 WHERE id = ?1` to `UPDATE quality_issues SET first_seen = datetime('now'), last_seen = datetime('now'), resolved = 0 WHERE id = ?1` (now overwrites first_seen on every re-observation). Targeted SA: SA2 (`first_seen UNCHANGED across re-observation`) — under the mutation the second `persist_diagnostics` call advances first_seen to `now2`, breaking the trend-tracking invariant. Restore: `git checkout -- crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/quality_pipeline.rs`. Pre-md5 path: `/tmp/wo-0085-quality-pipeline-orig.md5`.
